turnaround_reason: Stock crushed from $14+ highs to ~$6 on a growth deceleration — 2026 guided at 0-4% revenue growth and net loss in Q4 2025. Thesis: biosimulation/model-informed drug development (MIDD) is a structural winner with FDA tailwinds; AI-enabled product launches and cost discipline could stabilize margins in H2 2026. EQT Partners owns ~26% as PE anchor.
bullish_points: P/B 0.92x and forward P/E ~14.8x — cheap on an asset basis with real revenue ($420M TTM)
Software bookings grew 20% Y/Y in Q1 2026; company divesting lower-margin regulatory writing to shift mix toward higher-margin software
FDA MIDD (model-informed drug development) guidance is a structural tailwind — regulators increasingly requiring biosimulation
EQT Partners (26% owner) provides PE governance discipline; strategic review of regulatory writing unit could unlock value
Free cash flow of $117M TTM vs ~$950M market cap implies ~8% FCF yield
bearish_points: 2026 is explicitly a 'transition year' with 0-4% revenue growth guidance — execution risk is high
Net loss in Q4 ($5.9M) and Q1 ($8.8M); operating margins only 3.8% TTM
Services bookings down 14% Y/Y, and large pharma spending remains cautious
Short interest 28.6% of float — bears have a real thesis on growth stagnation
Options liquidity thin (344 call OI, 72 put OI) — limited for structured trades
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (Aug 6) — confirmation that software bookings acceleration continues and cost discipline is converting to margin expansion
suggested_trade: Spot only — options too illiquid. Scale in below $6, target $9-10 (1.5-1.7x P/B reversion) on margin recovery proof
key_risk: Large pharma clients continue deferring MIDD spending, software growth stalls, and FCF deteriorates rather than recovers
would_skip_if: If Q2 earnings show declining software bookings or further margin compression, the transition-year thesis is broken
